description: 55M, HTN, LDL 165, ASCVD risk 8.5%. Both USPSTF Grade C and ACC/AHA R4 independently recommend statin therapy. ACC/AHA provides more definitive COR I recommendation; USPSTF Grade C requires shared decision-making. Multi-guideline convergence on statin initiation.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-to-high-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ACC/AHA COR I, LOE A: adults 40-75 without diabetes, LDL 70-189 mg/dL, and 10-year ASCVD risk ≥7.5% should initiate moderate-to-high-intensity statin. USPSTF Grade C also recommends statin via shared decision-making for risk 7.5-<10%. Patient has LDL 165, ASCVD risk 8.5%. Two-guideline convergence strengthens recommendation.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 142/88 mmHg is above target despite HTN diagnosis. No current antihypertensive on medication list. HTN is a modifiable CVD risk factor.
